message:
[low] fcp-oauth: convert OAuth2/OAuth1 URL + signed-header contains() asserts to insta goldens

Four new golden snapshots in `tests/golden_snapshots.rs` replace
scattered `url.contains("...")` / `header.contains("...")` assertions
in `tests/no_mock_integration.rs` with whole-shape freeze-frames:

* `oauth2_authorization_url_basic_shape` — replaces the five
  `url.contains(...)` checks at no_mock_integration.rs:129-133 for
  `OAuth2Client::authorization_url`. Freezes the scheme + host +
  path plus every query parameter in alphabetical order. `state`
  (cryptographically random per call) is scrubbed to `<RANDOM>`;
  fixed-value params (`client_id`, `redirect_uri`, `response_type`,
  `scope`) are captured verbatim. Any change in parameter naming,
  ordering stability, absence, or addition of new defaults (e.g.,
  an injected `audience`) now shows up as a snapshot diff rather
  than silently passing the existing contains() checks.

* `oauth2_authorization_url_with_pkce_s256` — replaces the pair of
  `url.contains("code_challenge...")` checks at
  no_mock_integration.rs:151-152. `state` and `code_challenge` are
  both scrubbed; `code_challenge_method=S256` is captured verbatim
  so a regression to `plain` or a missing method param is caught.

* `oauth1_authorization_url_shape` — replaces
  no_mock_integration.rs:351-352 (`url.contains("oauth_token=...")`).
  No scrubbing needed; the OAuth 1.0a authorization URL carries
  only the caller-supplied request token.

* `oauth1_sign_request_header_shape` — replaces the seven
  `header.contains("oauth_...")` checks at
  no_mock_integration.rs:380-387 for
  `OAuth1Client::sign_request`. Freezes every field of the
  Authorization header, alphabetized. `oauth_nonce` and
  `oauth_timestamp` are scrubbed (random / wall-clock);
  `oauth_signature` is scrubbed because it depends on both. The
  fixed fields (`oauth_consumer_key`, `oauth_signature_method`,
  `oauth_token`, `oauth_version`) are captured verbatim.

Two small in-file normalizers keep the snapshots deterministic:
`normalize_authorization_url` parses the URL and emits
`scheme://host/path` followed by one `key = value` pair per line in
sorted order with the random fields scrubbed;
`normalize_oauth1_authorization_header` does the same for the
comma-separated `OAuth` header with quote-stripping. Neither uses
`insta::Settings::filters` because the scrub needs per-key
awareness rather than regex-level replacement.

Baseline confirms the pre-existing golden coverage was already
strong: `fcp_oauth::{refresh_request_*, response_*, redirect_*}`
snapshots (11 tests) were all green. This commit adds the
URL/header layer that was previously covered only by substring
asserts — the classic "present but wrong order / wrong encoding"
blind spot.

Verification:
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-p7-t13 cargo test -p fcp-oauth --test golden_snapshots
    → 15 passed; 0 failed (11 prior + 4 new).
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-p7-t13 cargo test -p fcp-oauth
    → 59 passed; 0 failed (unit + integration suite remains green).

fcp-host (the second half of the rotation target) does not
compile in the same target dir due to unrelated upstream breakage
— `RevocationFreshnessCheck` unresolved at multiple sites,
`ObjectHeader::metadata` field missing,
`EnforcementContextBuilder::token_id` method missing,
`RevocationObject::schema` missing. That's from another pane's
recent churn, not this patch. Skipping fcp-host per the
ship-or-surface protocol on the blocker; fcp-oauth coverage ships
standalone.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+---
+source: crates/fcp-oauth/tests/golden_snapshots.rs
+expression: normalize_oauth1_authorization_header(&header)
+---
+OAuth
+  oauth_consumer_key = consumer-key
+  oauth_nonce = <RANDOM>
+  oauth_signature = <RANDOM>
+  oauth_signature_method = HMAC-SHA1
+  oauth_timestamp = <RANDOM>
+  oauth_token = access-token
+  oauth_version = 1.0

@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+---
+source: crates/fcp-oauth/tests/golden_snapshots.rs
+expression: normalize_authorization_url(&url)
+---
+https://auth.ex.com/authorize
+  client_id = my-client
+  redirect_uri = https://localhost/cb
+  response_type = code
+  scope = read write
+  state = <RANDOM>

@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+---
+source: crates/fcp-oauth/tests/golden_snapshots.rs
+expression: normalize_authorization_url(&url)
+---
+https://auth.ex.com/authorize
+  client_id = id
+  code_challenge = <RANDOM>
+  code_challenge_method = S256
+  redirect_uri = https://localhost/cb
+  response_type = code
+  scope = openid
+  state = <RANDOM>
